$schema: Investor Profile - Individual High-Net-Worth (Accredited Investor, Direct or Syndication LP)

professionalCertification.note: Knowledgeable employees of a qualifying fund also qualify

status.verificationMethod: self-certification or third-party verification letter (CPA, attorney, broker-dealer)

offeringTypeAwareness.preferredOffering: 506(b) for relationship-sourced investments; 506(c) acceptable if GP has verified accreditation process

targetK1Count.description: Number of K-1s issued to investor per tax year across all real estate investments

k1BurdenManagement.flagAction: Alert investor and advisor when K-1 count would exceed 8 if new investment added; quantify incremental tax preparation cost

estimatedCostPerAdditionalK1.note: Incremental CPA cost per additional K-1 for a mid-complexity individual return

avoid: tiered entity structures with multiple pass-throughs

timing.extensionImpact: Late K-1s force investor extension; flag sponsors with history of late issuance

illiquidityRiskFlag.dealbreaker: Illiquid for > 7 years without exit mechanism

exitMechanisms.minimumRequired: at least one identified exit path beyond forced liquidation

forcedExitRisk.note: Individual investors may face personal liquidity events (health, divorce, job loss) that necessitate exit at non-optimal times
forcedExitRisk.mitigant: Prefer investments with secondary market viability or redemption rights

distributionPreference.deferralRisk: Flag if sponsor has history of distribution deferral without clear cure plan

sections.whatThisMeans: Every skill output must include a plain-language 'What this means for you' section
sections.keyAssumptions: All modeling assumptions must be listed and explained, not just stated
sections.glossaryEmbedded: Embed inline definitions for any term not in common financial vocabulary
sections.comparisonContext: Provide comparisons to familiar benchmarks (S&P 500, savings rate, bond yield) where applicable

conceptsToExplain: cap rate and why it matters
waterfall and promote mechanics (in LP investments)

outputSimplification.prefer: executive summary first, detail on request
outputSimplification.avoid: assumption tables without context, unexplained variance analysis

orchestratorPhaseModifications.marketResearch: Simplified output. Focus on 2-3 target markets with plain-language rationale. Skip macro MSA sweep -- go directly to 'is this market worth investing in for me?' framing.
orchestratorPhaseModifications.strategy: Return-focused with simple structures. Avoid complex JV waterfalls. Strategy should be explainable in a single paragraph. Prefer co-invest or direct ownership over fund structures.
orchestratorPhaseModifications.acquisition: Cap deal size to accredited investor suitability limits (max 15% of liquid net worth per deal). Simplified DD output with plain-language risk summary. Illiquidity period prominently flagged.
orchestratorPhaseModifications.assetManagement: Monthly one-page email summary. Plain-language metrics only. Annual in-person or video property update. No attribution analysis.
orchestratorPhaseModifications.portfolio: Single-asset or small-portfolio view (max 5-8 investments). No NCREIF benchmarking. Simple dashboard: what I own, what it earns, what it's worth.
orchestratorPhaseModifications.disposition: Explain tax implications in plain language with dollar amounts, not percentages only. 1031 education component mandatory. Model the after-tax proceeds scenario before recommending action.
orchestratorPhaseModifications.lpIntelligence: Primary use case for this investor type. Focus on fee transparency (total expense ratio), distribution track record, plain-language performance vs projections.

dealbreakers: Personal guaranty exposure > 2x liquid net worth
K-1 complexity exceeding tax advisor capacity
Illiquid for > 7 years without exit mechanism
Total CRE exposure > 40% of net worth
Single investment > 15% of liquid net worth
Sponsor without track record of on-time K-1 issuance
Complex multi-tier entity structure with 3+ K-1 layers
No identified exit path beyond hope-and-hold liquidation
